reasoning: The forecast horizon for this question is extremely short, spanning only about two months (April 25, 2025 to July 1, 2025). The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) rarely changes its components, historically averaging a change every few years to keep index turnover low. The index just underwent two major discretionary reshuffles in 2024 (adding Amazon in February 2024, and adding NVIDIA and Sherwin-Williams in November 2024). Having a third reshuffle within such a short timeframe would be unprecedented unless forced by a major corporate action like a buyout or bankruptcy. Currently, none of the 30 DJIA constituents are targets of acquisitions, and there are no imminent bankruptcies. Previously struggling stocks like Intel and Walgreens have already been pruned. While Boeing is facing challenges, it survived worse drops in 2020 without being removed. Thus, the index committee is highly unlikely to announce any further component removals in this narrow two-month window. Following the rule to keep probabilities bounded, I estimate a 5% chance.
